1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
2	// Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors
3	//! Slice-3.1b userland bootstrap: per-process address spaces with 8-bit
4	//! ARMv8 ASIDs.
5	//!
6	//! Each of the three EL0 stubs (A, B, C — the slice 2.5 / 2.6 regression
7	//! coverage) gets its own [`AddrSpace`] built from the slice-3.1a frame
8	//! allocator. Code and stack pages are allocated as fresh frames, the
9	//! stub blob is copied in via HHDM (`mm::phys_to_hhdm`), and the
10	//! resulting `(ttbr0_pa, asid)` is stored on the proc slot. The scheduler
11	//! ([`crate::proc::sched::schedule_next`]) installs that TTBR0 on every
12	//! EL1 → EL0 transition.
13	//!
14	//! What slice 3.1a's smoke test exercised in isolation — `AddrSpace::new`,
15	//! `map_page`, `walk_pt`, the free-list reuse on `destroy` — now drives
16	//! the three real EL0 stubs.
17	//!
18	//! What this slice does *not* do:
19	//!   - Recover from page faults. EC=0x20 / EC=0x24 still panic via
20	//!     [`super::exception::el0_sync_unexpected`]; slice 3.1b extends that
21	//!     handler's ISS decoder so the dump is informative, but the policy
22	//!     is still "halt" — real `do_page_fault` + `RTS_PAGEFAULT` land in
23	//!     slice 3.2.
24	//!   - Cross-AS IPC delivery. The SVC handler runs in the caller's TTBR0,
25	//!     so single-AS reads in `ipc::message` still walk correctly; slice
26	//!     3.4's HHDM-walk redesign of `flush_deliver_msg` swaps that out
27	//!     once VM exists.
28	//!   - Install a kernel-shared global mapping for the EL0 stubs. The
29	//!     code page is mapped RO + EL0-executable into each per-proc AS
30	//!     individually (zero shared frames).
31	
32	use core::sync::atomic::Ordering;
33	
34	use minixrs_kernel_shared::callnr::{KERNEL_CALL, SYS_GETINFO};
35	use minixrs_kernel_shared::com::{RS_PROC_NR, SCHED_PROC_NR, SYSTEM, VM_PROC_NR, boot_endpoint};
36	use minixrs_kernel_shared::{PrivId, ProcNr};
37	
38	use crate::arch::aarch64::addrspace::{AddrSpace, Prot};
39	use crate::arch::aarch64::asid::alloc_asid;
40	use crate::arch::aarch64::mmu::{self, PAGE_SIZE, flush_icache_range};
41	use crate::mm::{Frame, alloc_frame, phys_to_hhdm};
42	use crate::proc::bitmap::{set_call_bit, set_sys_bit};
43	use crate::proc::flags::{BILLABLE, PREEMPTIBLE, SRV_T, SYS_PROC, USR_T};
44	use crate::proc::sched;
45	use crate::proc::table::{priv_slot_mut, proc_index, proc_slot_mut, proc_table_ref};
46	use crate::proc::{HeapWindow, Priv, Proc};
47	
48	// ----- EL0 virtual addresses ------------------------------------------------
49	
50	/// VA at which stub A's code page is mapped.
51	pub const USER_CODE_VA_A: u64 = 0x0040_0000; // 4 MiB
52	/// VA at which stub A's stack page is mapped.
53	pub const USER_STACK_VA_A: u64 = 0x0080_0000; // 8 MiB
54	
55	/// VA at which stub B's code page is mapped.
56	pub const USER_CODE_VA_B: u64 = 0x0041_0000;
57	/// VA at which stub B's stack page is mapped.
58	pub const USER_STACK_VA_B: u64 = 0x0081_0000;
59	
60	/// VA at which stub C's code page is mapped.
61	pub const USER_CODE_VA_C: u64 = 0x0042_0000;
62	/// VA at which stub C's stack page is mapped.
63	pub const USER_STACK_VA_C: u64 = 0x0082_0000;
64	
65	/// VA at which stub D's code page is mapped.
66	pub const USER_CODE_VA_D: u64 = 0x0043_0000;
67	/// VA at which stub D's stack page is mapped.
68	pub const USER_STACK_VA_D: u64 = 0x0083_0000;
69	
70	// Stub D's heap VA (`0x0100_0000`) lives only in `user_stub.S`'s stub D blob
71	// and in the VM server's `region::HEAP_BASE` (slice 3.5): D issues `VM_BRK` to
72	// grow its heap, then touches it. The kernel needs no heap-window constant —
73	// faults route to VM, which gates them on its per-proc region table.
74	
75	/// First proc-table slot beyond the boot image — `ProcNr(11)`.
76	const STUB_A_PROC_NR: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(11);
77	/// Second stub slot, just after A.
78	const STUB_B_PROC_NR: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(12);
79	/// Slice-2.6 kernel-call client. Slot 13 — one past the slice-2.5 pair.
80	const STUB_C_PROC_NR: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(13);
81	/// Slice-3.2 page-fault demo. Slot 14.
82	const STUB_D_PROC_NR: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(14);
83	
84	/// Privilege-table slots for the four stubs. Boot image uses 0..=15;
85	/// 16, 17, 18, and 19 are the first free entries.
86	const STUB_A_PRIV_ID: PrivId = PrivId::new(16);
87	const STUB_B_PRIV_ID: PrivId = PrivId::new(17);
88	const STUB_C_PRIV_ID: PrivId = PrivId::new(18);
89	const STUB_D_PRIV_ID: PrivId = PrivId::new(19);
90	
91	// SPSR_EL1 to install on each stub's `eret`. Matches slice 2.4: IRQs
92	// unmasked at EL0 (bit 7 = 0), debug + SError + FIQ masked. EL0t.
93	const STUB_SPSR_EL0: u64 = 0x340;
94	
95	// ----- EL0 stub blobs (linked into the kernel image by `user_stub.S`) ------
96	
97	unsafe extern "C" {
98	    static _user_stub_a_start: u8;
99	    static _user_stub_a_end: u8;
100	    static _user_stub_b_start: u8;
101	    static _user_stub_b_end: u8;
102	    static _user_stub_c_start: u8;
103	    static _user_stub_c_end: u8;
104	    static _user_stub_d_start: u8;
105	    static _user_stub_d_end: u8;
106	}
107	
108	/// VA at which each boot server's stack page is mapped. Distinct from the server
109	/// ELF segments (loaded from `0x0010_0000` up by `servers/*/user.ld`) and from
110	/// every EL0 stub VA. Shared across all servers: each has its own per-process
111	/// TTBR0, so the same low VA resolves to a distinct frame per server with no
112	/// collision (the same reason `user.ld`'s `0x0010_0000` base is shared).
113	const SERVER_STACK_VA: u64 = 0x0020_0000; // 2 MiB
114	
115	// ----- Bootstrap ----------------------------------------------------------
116	
117	/// Build three per-process address spaces (one per EL0 stub), populate
118	/// their proc + priv slots, and enqueue them on the scheduler.
119	///
120	/// Compared to slice 2.5/2.6 this:
121	///   - Drops the static `L0_TABLE`/.../`L3_STACK_TABLE` + `USER_*_PAGE_*`
122	///     arrays. Every frame is allocated from `mm::alloc_frame`.
123	///   - Hands each stub its own `(ttbr0_pa, asid)` via
124	///     [`build_stub`] + [`AddrSpace`].
125	///   - No longer installs a single TTBR0 from boot context; the scheduler
126	///     installs per-proc TTBR0 + ASID on every EL1 → EL0 transition.
127	///
128	/// SAFETY: must be called exactly once from `kmain` after `proc::init`,
129	/// `mm::set_hhdm_offset`, and `mm::init_from_limine_memmap`. Single-
130	/// threaded boot context; no other reference into the named proc slots
131	/// or the frame allocator may be live concurrently.
132	pub unsafe fn userland_bootstrap() {
133	    // 1. Verify Limine left MAIR_EL1 + TCR_EL1 in the shape our walker
134	    //    + per-proc TTBR0 install expect. The slice-3.1b TCR_EL1.AS = 0
135	    //    assert was added inside `assert_tcr_el1_ttbr0_ready`.
136	    mmu::assert_mair_normal_wb();
137	    mmu::assert_tcr_el1_ttbr0_ready();
138	
139	    // 2. Clear TCR_EL1.EPD0 once. Per-proc TTBR0 install happens at every
140	    //    context switch via `proc::sched::schedule_next`.
141	    // SAFETY: DAIF still masked from Limine handoff; single-threaded boot.
142	    unsafe { mmu::enable_ttbr0_walks_once() };
143	
144	    // 2.5. Load every boot server from the embedded MXBI archive (slice 4.2).
145	    //      VM is packed first so it takes ASID 1 and is enqueued first; its
146	    //      `RECEIVE(ANY)` blocks immediately (no senders yet), leaving the run
147	    //      queue free for the band-8 stubs. Each server's proc/priv slot was
148	    //      already populated by `proc::init` → `init_boot_image`; this fills in
149	    //      the address space + EL0 entry state, clears RTS_NO_PRIV, and enqueues.
150	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; sole writer of each server's proc slot + the
151	    // frame allocator.
152	    unsafe {
153	        let image = crate::boot_image::BootImage::get();
154	        for (nr, elf) in image.iter() {
155	            load_boot_server(nr, elf, SERVER_STACK_VA);
156	        }
157	    }
158	
159	    // 3. Build each stub. Sequential calls — each build_stub allocates
160	    //    fresh frames + a fresh AddrSpace and writes its proc slot.
161	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; build_stub only touches its own
162	    // allocated state + the named proc slot.
163	    unsafe {
164	        build_stub(
165	            STUB_A_PROC_NR,
166	            STUB_A_PRIV_ID,
167	            b'A',
168	            &_user_stub_a_start,
169	            &_user_stub_a_end,
170	            USER_CODE_VA_A,
171	            USER_STACK_VA_A,
172	            HeapWindow::EMPTY,
173	        );
174	        build_stub(
175	            STUB_B_PROC_NR,
176	            STUB_B_PRIV_ID,
177	            b'B',
178	            &_user_stub_b_start,
179	            &_user_stub_b_end,
180	            USER_CODE_VA_B,
181	            USER_STACK_VA_B,
182	            HeapWindow::EMPTY,
183	        );
184	        build_stub(
185	            STUB_C_PROC_NR,
186	            STUB_C_PRIV_ID,
187	            b'C',
188	            &_user_stub_c_start,
189	            &_user_stub_c_end,
190	            USER_CODE_VA_C,
191	            USER_STACK_VA_C,
192	            HeapWindow::EMPTY,
193	        );
194	        build_stub(
195	            STUB_D_PROC_NR,
196	            STUB_D_PRIV_ID,
197	            b'D',
198	            &_user_stub_d_start,
199	            &_user_stub_d_end,
200	            USER_CODE_VA_D,
201	            USER_STACK_VA_D,
202	            // D issues `VM_BRK` to grow its heap and touches it (slice 3.5),
203	            // then `VM_MMAP`/`VM_MUNMAP` an anonymous region (slice 3.6). Faults
204	            // route to the VM server, which gates them on its region table. The
205	            // kernel `heap_window` fast path stays unused, so EMPTY.
206	            HeapWindow::EMPTY,
207	        );
208	    }
209	
210	    // 3.5. Pre-delegate stub C to the user-space SCHED server (slice 4.3). C is
211	    //      a CPU-bound SYS_GETINFO loop, so it regularly exhausts its quantum,
212	    //      exercising the kernel → SCHED `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` → `SYS_SCHEDULE`
213	    //      round-trip. Every other proc stays kernel-scheduled (`scheduler ==
214	    //      NONE`), including SCHED itself (a scheduler must not schedule itself).
215	    //      This stands in for the `SCHEDULING_START` a real RS/PM will issue once
216	    //      they exist (slice 4.5/4.6); until then the kernel claims C directly.
217	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; sole borrow of stub C's slot.
218	    unsafe {
219	        let p = proc_slot_mut(STUB_C_PROC_NR).expect("stub C proc slot in range");
220	        p.scheduler = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR);
221	    }
222	
223	    // 4. Install priv slots — unchanged from slice 2.6.
224	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; sequential mutable borrows on
225	    // priv-table slots 16, 17, 18.
226	    unsafe { install_stub_privs() };
227	
228	    // 5. Enqueue all three on the scheduler.
229	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; no other PROC_TABLE / RUNQ borrows
230	    // live at this point.
231	    unsafe {
232	        sched::enqueue(STUB_A_PROC_NR);
233	        sched::enqueue(STUB_B_PROC_NR);
234	        sched::enqueue(STUB_C_PROC_NR);
235	        sched::enqueue(STUB_D_PROC_NR);
236	    }
237	
238	    // 6. One-shot diagnostic: dump per-proc (ttbr0_pa, asid) so the
239	    //    boot log proves each stub has its own distinct AS.
240	    //    SAFETY: single-threaded boot; read-only.
241	    unsafe { print_addrspace_summary() };
242	}
243	
244	/// Load one boot server's ELF (`elf`) into a fresh address space, prime its
245	/// entry/stack registers, clear the boot `RTS_NO_PRIV` block, and enqueue it.
246	/// Generalizes the slice-3.4 `vm_bootstrap` over the MXBI archive (slice 4.2).
247	///
248	/// Unlike the hand-coded stubs (which use ad-hoc proc/priv slots), a boot server
249	/// occupies its *real* boot slot `nr`: `proc::init` → `init_boot_image` already
250	/// populated its name, priority, endpoint, and privilege slot (`trap_mask =
251	/// SRV_T` → RECEIVE ANY + SEND to any active slot; full `k_call_mask`). So this
252	/// fills in only the address space + EL0 entry state. No `install_*_priv` helper
253	/// is needed.
254	///
255	/// `stack_va` is shared across servers ([`SERVER_STACK_VA`]); each gets its own
256	/// TTBR0, so the same VA maps a distinct frame per server.
257	///
258	/// The `AddrSpace` is `mem::forget`-ed for the same reason as [`build_stub`]:
259	/// the page-table tree is now owned via `Proc::ttbr0_pa`.
260	///
261	/// SAFETY: single-threaded boot; the only writer of `nr`'s proc slot and the
262	/// frame allocator here. Must run after `mm::init_from_limine_memmap` and
263	/// before `sched::run`. `nr` must be a boot server already populated by
264	/// `init_boot_image` (blocked on `RTS_NO_PRIV`).
265	unsafe fn load_boot_server(nr: ProcNr, elf: &[u8], stack_va: u64) {
266	    use crate::arch::aarch64::uart::Pl011;
267	    use core::fmt::Write;
268	
269	    let mut aspace = AddrSpace::new().expect("server AddrSpace::new failed");
270	    let ttbr0_pa = aspace.ttbr0_pa;
271	
272	    let entry =
273	        crate::boot_image::elf::load_into(elf, &mut aspace).expect("server ELF load failed");
274	
275	    // Stack: one zeroed RW page; SP starts at its top.
276	    let stack_frame = alloc_frame().expect("server stack frame alloc failed");
277	    aspace
278	        .map_page(stack_va, stack_frame.addr(), Prot::RW_DATA)
279	        .expect("map_page(server stack) failed");
280	
281	    let asid = unsafe { alloc_asid() };
282	
283	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; sole borrow of this server's slot.
284	    unsafe {
285	        let p = proc_slot_mut(nr).expect("server proc slot in range");
286	        p.regs.elr_el1 = entry;
287	        p.regs.sp_el0 = stack_va + PAGE_SIZE as u64;
288	        p.regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0;
289	        p.ttbr0_pa = ttbr0_pa;
290	        p.asid = asid;
291	        p.next_ready = None;
292	        // Clear the boot RTS_NO_PRIV: the server now has an address space.
293	        p.rts_flags.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
294	
295	        let name = core::str::from_utf8(&p.name)
296	            .unwrap_or("?")
297	            .trim_end_matches('\0');
298	        let _ = writeln!(
299	            Pl011::new(),
300	            "[as] {} nr={} ttbr0_pa={:#x} asid={} entry={:#x}",
301	            name,
302	            p.nr.get(),
303	            p.ttbr0_pa,
304	            p.asid,
305	            entry,
306	        );
307	    }
308	
309	    // The page-table tree is durable via `ttbr0_pa`; don't run `destroy`.
310	    core::mem::forget(aspace);
311	
312	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; no other PROC_TABLE / RUNQ borrow is live.
313	    unsafe { sched::enqueue(nr) };
314	}
315	
316	/// Build one stub's address space: allocate L0 + code + stack frames,
317	/// copy the stub blob into the code frame, install RX/RW mappings, and
318	/// write `(ttbr0_pa, asid)` into the proc slot.
319	///
320	/// `aspace` is intentionally `mem::forget`-ed at the end: the page-table
321	/// tree is now owned by the proc (via its `ttbr0_pa`); the kernel will
322	/// only reach into it again via HHDM walks. `AddrSpace::destroy` would
323	/// recursively free every L1/L2/L3 frame plus the L0 root, which is
324	/// exactly what we don't want.
325	///
326	/// SAFETY: single-threaded boot; this is the only writer of `nr`'s proc
327	/// slot. `stub_start..stub_end` must be a valid contiguous rodata range
328	/// in the kernel image; the linker guarantees both.

*   7. VM_MUNMAP(x22, 0x2000): SENDREC to VM. VM drops the region and unmaps the
 *      one mapped page (SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_UNMAP)); the second, never-touched
 *      page is a harmless EINVAL inside the kernel.
 *   8. Loop writing the two *heap* pages, incrementing a counter in x19. The
 *      mmap page is now unmapped, so D must not touch x22 again (that would be
 *      an out-of-region SIGSEGV). No further faults — D round-robins like A/B/C.
 *
 * x19/x20/x21/x22 are AAPCS callee-saved and survive the SVC (trap.S saves and
 * restores x0..x30), so x22 (the mmap base) is still valid for the munmap after
 * an intervening SVC. The 104-byte Message is carved from [SP_EL0-104, SP_EL0).
 */

/* ---- Stub E: PM getpid client (slice 4.5) ------------------------------- */

/*
 * Stub E is built *frozen*: `userland_bootstrap` constructs its address space
 * but installs no privilege slot and leaves RTS_NO_PRIV set — the state a
 * forked child will hold in 4.6. PM's SEF init releases it with
 * SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER), pointing it at the shared USER priv slot
 * (SENDREC-to-PM only, no kernel calls). E then loops SENDREC PM_GETPID to
 * PM: the reply's m_type is E's pid and payload[0..4] the parent's pid — the
 * live proof that a PM-granted privilege works end to end.
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_e, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_e_start
    .type _user_stub_e_start, %object
_user_stub_e_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent call counter
1:
    // Build the request at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0):
    //   [+0]   m_source — kernel overwrites
    //   [+4]   m_type = PM_GETPID (no request payload)
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
    mov     w10, #PM_GETPID
    str     w10, [x9, #4]
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_PM
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_e_end
_user_stub_e_end:
    .size _user_stub_e_start, _user_stub_e_end - _user_stub_e_start

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "phase 4.5: stub E (frozen) + stub D poison + VM raises SIGSEGV

Stub E (proc 15) is built with a full address space but no priv slot and
RTS_NO_PRIV set (build_stub gains Option<PrivId> + frozen) - the state a
forked child holds in 4.6; PM will release it via PRIVCTL_SET_USER. Its
blob loops SENDREC PM_GETPID to PM. Stub D's steady loop now runs 32
iterations then deliberately stores to the munmapped mmap page: VM's
region check fails and, instead of the slice-3.5 silent return, VM
raises SYS_KILL(faulter, SIGSEGV) toward PM. Live intermediate state
(PM not yet packed): 10 [as] lines, D's 3 resolved [pf] + 1 fatal,
[ksys SYS_KILL] target=D sig=11, ksig NOTIFY deferred into dormant PM's
notify_pending, zero panic.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "phase 4.5: PM server — mproc + getpid + ksig drain + stub E release

servers/pm becomes a real SEF boot server (MXBI row 6, proc 0): pm_init
publishes to DS, seeds the host-tested mproc table (pids: PM 0, INIT 1,
servers 2..10 in slot order parented to RS, stubs 11..15 parented to
INIT), and releases the frozen stub E via SYS_PRIVCTL(SET_USER). The
loop serves PM_GETPID (reply m_type = pid, ppid in payload 0..4) and,
on the kernel's NOTIFY-from-SYSTEM, drains ksigs: GETKSIG -> default
terminate (SYS_EXIT; boot servers skipped) -> ALWAYS ENDKSIG. RS
heartbeats PM as a 5th peer; classify gains a test pinning NOTIFY-from-
SYSTEM as Application; ipc TRACE_HEAD 12 -> 24 for six-server boot
chatter.

Verified in QEMU over 25 s: eleven [as] (six servers asid 1-6, stubs
A-E 7-11); [ksys SYS_PRIVCTL] target=E exactly once; E's PM_GETPID
SENDRECs recur in sampled [ipc] (caller=15 -> PM reply to 0xf); D's
three resolved [pf] + one fatal, then the full chain SYS_KILL sig=11 ->
GETKSIG map=0x800 -> SYS_EXIT -> ENDKSIG and D goes silent; A/B
ping-pong, C [noq]/SYS_SCHEDULE, six RS [alarm] fires all intact; zero
panic; every traced result=0.
